evals.prompt: My training finished. How do I download the weights, merge the LoRA adapter, and publish to HuggingFace?
expectations: Uses weights.download() with a tinker:// path
Uses weights.build_hf_model() to merge LoRA into base model
Uses weights.publish_to_hf_hub() with repo_id parameter
Mentions that download expects sampler weights (save_weights_for_sampler), not state checkpoints
Mentions setting HF_TOKEN for publishing
Mentions weights.build_lora_adapter() as an alternative for serving with vLLM/SGLang
Recommends dtype='bfloat16' for the merge

evals.prompt: My RL training seems stuck. How do I analyze the training logs to figure out what's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Guide to reading metrics.jsonl, checking reward curves, KL divergence, and rollout summaries
expectations: Shows how to load metrics.jsonl with pandas or json
Mentions checking env/all/reward/total for reward progress
Mentions checking KL divergence (kl_sample_train) — should stay below 0.01
Suggests looking at rollout summaries in iteration_NNNNNN/train_rollout_summaries.jsonl
Suggests practical fixes: lower learning rate if KL is spiking, check reward parsing if stuck at 0
Mentions the HTML reports (train.html) for human-readable rollout inspection
Mentions shrinking workloads (batch_size, group_size, max_steps) for debugging

evals.prompt: How do I set up inline evaluation during training so I can track accuracy on a test set?
evals.expected_output: Evaluator setup using evaluator_builders in training config
expectations: Shows how to create an evaluator function that takes a SamplingClient and returns metrics dict
Shows how to add it to the training config via evaluator_builders
Mentions eval_every parameter to control evaluation frequency
Mentions infrequent_evaluator_builders for expensive evals
Mentions Inspect AI integration as an option for standard benchmarks
Shows the RL test set pattern (RLDatasetBuilder returning optional test_dataset)

evals.prompt: I need to evaluate my trained model on 200 test problems during training. How do I write the evaluator so it doesn't slow down training?
evals.expected_output: Async evaluator using concurrent execution for test problems
expectations: Evaluates test problems concurrently using asyncio.gather or similar — NOT sequentially
Uses infrequent_evaluator_builders for expensive evals to avoid running every step
Creates samples with SamplingClient.sample() or a completer
Mentions keeping evaluators fast to avoid stalling the training loop
Does not evaluate problems in a sequential for loop one-by-one
